mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is flat or falling, discount rates ease and equity valuation multiples expand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is invalid if rate cuts coincide with rising inflation that forces the market to reprice policy higher.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are negative in a majority of periods where FEDFUNDS is declining or unchanged and CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income growth, which lowers expected corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the average forward return is not negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A broad acceleration in RSAFS together with rising HOUST indicates stronger final demand and housing-linked spending, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and rising HOUST does not outperform either series alone, or if the conditional 20-day return spread versus baseline is not positive over any 2-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in INDPRO signals improving industrial output and easing supply constraints, which supports earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases versus its value 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if upward INDPRO momentum is followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is consistently below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, policy is restrictive enough to compress equity multiples and weaken risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days are negative unless inflation is falling rapidly enough to imply imminent easing.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate trigger periods deliver positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if adding CPIAUCSL does not improve classification versus FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS count after prior gains indicates payroll rollover and weaker aggregate income growth, which lowers earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month after a prior 3-month rise
failure_condition: Reject if payroll declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in most triggered cases, or if the signal loses sign stability in any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth and retail sales both improve, the market reads it as synchronized demand strength, which raises expected earnings and lifts S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The mechanism fails if growth is driven only by price inflation rather than real activity.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns more often than the unconditional baseline, or if the pairwise interaction term is not significant out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO combined with stable or falling UNRATE signals output reacceleration without labor-market damage, which supports margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising INDPRO and non-rising UNRATE fails to outperform either signal alone, or if triggered 20-day returns are not positive in at least 55% of cases.
